channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Southern Poverty Law Center indicted on federal fraud charges
item.description: The Justice Department alleges that the SPLC improperly raised millions of dollars to pay informants to infiltrate the Ku Klux Klan and other extremist groups.
item.content:encoded:
The Justice Department alleges that the SPLC improperly raised millions of dollars to pay informants to infiltrate the Ku Klux Klan and other extremist groups.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump administration gives an update on its plans to overhaul air traffic control
item.description: Nearly a year after launching an ambitious plan to rebuild the U.S.'s air traffic control infrastructure, leaders at the DOT gave an update, including details on a proposed AI-driven software system.
item.content:encoded: Nearly a year after launching an ambitious plan to rebuild the U.S.'s air traffic control infrastructure, leaders at the DOT gave an update, including details on a proposed AI-driven software system.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Florida AG launches criminal investigation into ChatGPT over FSU shooting
item.description: Florida's attorney general is launching a criminal investigation into the alleged role of ChatGPT in a mass shooting at Florida State University last year.
item.content:encoded:
Florida's attorney general is launching a criminal investigation into the alleged role of ChatGPT in a mass shooting at Florida State University last year.

(Image credit: Miguel J. Rodriguez Carrillo)
item.dc:creator: Tristan Wood

item.title: Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran at Pakistan's request
item.description: The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.
item.content:encoded:
The extension was announced just hours before it was set to expire. The president did not provide details on how long the new ceasefire extension will last.

(Image credit: Aamir Qureshi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: After deadly Louisiana shooting, a doctor shares the realities of domestic violence
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with gun violence expert and emergency physician Dr. Megan Ranney about the mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with gun violence expert and emergency physician Dr. Megan Ranney about the mass shooting in Shreveport, Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: As U.S.-Iran ceasefire comes to an end, talks to extend the agreement remain unclear, Trump's labor secretary resigns, Fed chair nominee goes before a Senate committee.
item.content:encoded: As U.S.-Iran ceasefire comes to an end, talks to extend the agreement remain unclear, Trump's labor secretary resigns, Fed chair nominee goes before a Senate committee.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: 2 boys killed in 'planned fight' that escalated, North Carolina police say
item.description: Authorities in Winston-Salem, N.C. are investigating the shooting deaths of two boys, and the wounding of several others. Police described it as a "planned fight" at a park that escalated.
item.content:encoded: Authorities in Winston-Salem, N.C. are investigating the shooting deaths of two boys, and the wounding of several others. Police described it as a "planned fight" at a park that escalated.
item.dc:creator: David Ford

item.title: Community grapples with the deaths of 8 children in domestic violence incident
item.description: Authorities in Shreveport, Louisiana, continue investigating the shooting deaths of eight children. Police say the killings were tied to domestic violence.
item.content:encoded: Authorities in Shreveport, Louisiana, continue investigating the shooting deaths of eight children. Police say the killings were tied to domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Ferrell

item.title: The Onion has agreed to a new deal to take over Infowars
item.description: The Onion says it has a new deal to take over conspiracy theorist Alex Jones' Infowars media company. If approved, the satirical news website could turn Infowars into a parody of itself.
item.content:encoded:
The Onion says it has a new deal to take over conspiracy theorist Alex Jones' Infowars media company. If approved, the satirical news website could turn Infowars into a parody of itself.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Tovia Smith

item.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO. In letter, describes 15 years of emails
item.description: The 65-year-old Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware products, John Ternus, in September. Cook will remain with the company as executive chairman.
item.content:encoded:
The 65-year-old Cook will turn over CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware products, John Ternus, in September. Cook will remain with the company as executive chairman.

(Image credit: Godofredo A. Vásquez)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch
